title: Lowered upstream timeout config turns slow-but-OK into 504s
summary: An api-gateway config change lowers the upstream request timeout from 5000ms to 500ms. The catalog service is healthy but routinely answers in 300-700ms, so requests above 500ms are now aborted as 504s. There is no code deploy and catalog's own latency is unchanged; the cause is a non-deploy configuration change.
question: Why did api-gateway start returning 504s on catalog routes around 11:20 on 2026-06-12 with no deploy?

ground_truth.notes: catalog latency is unchanged and its server spans complete successfully; the gateway aborts them at the new 500ms deadline. Change-ingestion must capture config changes, not just deploys, and the healthy-downstream signal rules catalog out.
